programme.desc: When objects start to go missing from her home, Sophie Hartley (Susan Sarandon) suspects that a stalker is responsible. Then she meets Mara (Emily Blunt), the new co-worker of her husband, Craig (Sam Neill), and after seeing her in a dress that was among the vanished items, Sophie believes that Mara is obsessed with her. Unfortunately, she takes her suspicions too far, which leads to legal ramifications and her husband becoming concerned for her well-being -- but also to a startling discovery.

programme.desc: Set in North Carolina, "A Walk To Remember" follows the rite of passage of a jaded, aimless high school senior (Shane West) who falls in love with a guileless young woman (Mandy Moore) he and his friends once scorned. The two develop a powerful and inspirational relationship in which they discover truths that take most people a lifetime to learn.

programme.desc: Two female survivors join forces to bring down the deadly group responsible for unleashing a hungry shark in a West Texas lake.

programme.title: Lone Star Shark 2
programme.desc: A new victim has been chosen to face off against a ravenous shark in a Texas lake.

programme.title: Furie

programme.desc: Author Cheryln Cadle divulges chilling never-before-heard details about Watts.

programme.title: The Boy Who Vanished
programme.desc: A mother's long-lost son returns a decade after his abduction.

programme.title: Snatched from the Crib
programme.desc: Gabi's search for her missing pregnant stepsister takes a chilling turn.

programme.title: Cradle of Lies
programme.desc: Marissa was in a coma while pregnant and wakes up five years later to find that the baby she delivered is gone

programme.title: Snowman's Pass
programme.desc: Impresario aids fiancée in search for her fallen climber, masking his true goal: finding a spy satellite.

programme.title: Best Friend From Heaven
programme.desc: When her dog is killed, Tara cancels her wedding. But her dog's ghost and her small town have a surprise.

programme.title: The Secrets of Comfort House
programme.desc: A murder investigation in a small town focuses on Wendy Sinclair, who runs a shelter for the victims' wives.

programme.title: Maternal Instinct
programme.desc: Teen witnesses mother's shooting, hides with a cop, facing danger as they evade the killer together.

programme.title: The Climb
programme.desc: The breath-taking story of two men ... one with a courageous heart who seeks God; the other with an independent spirit who relies on his own strength.

programme.title: The Challenger
programme.desc: The Challenger follows the life struggles of a young, Bronx auto mechanic, Jaden Miller. When Jaden and his mother are evicted from their home, he soon finds that boxing may be the key to stop them from living on the streets and the ticket to a better life. With legendary trainer, Duane Taylor, Miller becomes a local hero and captures the attention of a cable network that decides to create a reality show around his goal to fight to the Light-Heavyweight Title.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: The Moon is in Aquarius
programme.desc: On a starry night lit by an Aquarius full moon, Devante and Liz collide at a house party in San Francisco. The spark is instant-and impossible to ignore. Ditching the crowd, they embark on a spontaneous journey through the city's winding streets, iconic views, and hidden gems. With every laugh and shared secret, their bond deepens. But is this love story bound by time-do they only have until dawn?

programme.title: I'm In Love With A Church Girl
programme.desc: A reformed drug trafficker finds unexpected love with a woman guided by strong faith, testing his resolve to leave his criminal past behind.

programme.title: Women of the Jury
programme.desc: A man is on trial accused of murder of two young black men, and twelve women are called to serve on the jury. After deliberation, one juror is convinced the man is not guilty, while the majority claims he is guilty. A conversation takes place consisting of arguments, alliances, and near violence. What will the verdict be?

programme.title: Liquid Dreams
programme.desc: The Dawkins brothers, navigating new careers and relationships, find their bond tested when a wild night of partying sets off a chain of events. Years after their initial adventures, they face fresh challenges that threaten to tear them apart.

programme.title: Double Blended
programme.desc: Two couples, once married to each other’s ex-spouses, form a double-blended family. Their seemingly harmonious coexistence is threatened when a past secret resurfaces, forcing them to confront their complicated relationships.

programme.title: Counterplay
programme.desc: Antwan Walker and his beautiful wife Nia have the perfect marriage. When Antwan’s wandering eye leads him to flirt with his co-worker Monica, what follows is a psychological thriller as Antwan slowly realizes that Monica has a hidden agenda.

programme.title: Father Knows Best
programme.desc: Zoe Miles, raised by a controlling father who introduces her to a life of crime, grapples with the lingering trauma of her mother's death. Yearning to embrace the spiritual life she shared with her grandmother, Zoe finds herself trapped in a cycle of violence, forced to rob drug dealers after witnessing a terrifying crime. Desperate to escape this destructive path, she struggles against her father's suffocating control, fearing the loss of his approval and the validation she desperately seeks.

programme.title: Apocalypse Pompeii
programme.desc: When Mt. Vesuvius erupts, a vacationing former SPECIAL OPS commando must rescue his trapped wife and daughter.

programme.title: Ice Sharks
programme.desc: As scientists study the effects of climate change on sea life, a new breed of shark breaks through.

programme.title: 5-Headed Shark Attack
programme.desc: A monster 5-Headed Shark terrorizes the beaches of Puerto Rico, endangering the once peaceful island paradise.

programme.title: The Land That Time Forgot
programme.desc: A modern adaptation of the classic novel about survivors of a shipwreck trapped on an island with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Disaster Strike Force
programme.desc: The team responds to the threat of a potentially devastating earthquake in Los Angeles.

programme.title: Allan Quatermain and the Temple of Skulls
programme.desc: A retelling of Haggard’s classic novel featuring Allan Quartermain, the inspiration for Indiana Jones.

programme.title: Elvira's Movie Macabre: The Werewolf Of Washington
programme.desc: Elvira presents the 1973 Horror-Comedy The Werewolf of Washington which acts as a political spoof of The Wolf Man.

programme.title: Ghosthouse
programme.desc: A group of unlikely companions receive a radio call leading to a deserted house with a grisly past. After exploring the house, the group makes the foolish decision to split up, leading to a trail of death and destruction as the house unleashes its deadly past. Along the way expect severed heads, exploding light bulbs, demonic clown dolls, and creepy children. The clown doll is the main evil force from the film; the director of a funeral home stole it out of a child's coffin to give to his own daughter, leading to angry spirits possessing both the girl and the clown doll.

programme.title: November
programme.desc: NOVEMBER is set in a pagan Estonian village where werewolves, the plague, and spirits roam, Rainer Sarnets third feature film is a bold, twisted fairy tale about unrequited love. In NOVEMBER, the villagers main problem is how to survive the cold, dark winter. And, to that aim, nothing is taboo. People steal from each other, from their German manor lords, from spirits, the devil, and from Christ. They are willing to give away their souls to thieving creatures made of wood and metal called kratts, who help their masters, whose soul they purchased, steal even more. A young farmgirl Liina (Rea Lest) is hopelessly in love with Hans (Jrgen Liik), a nearby farmhand, whose heart she loses to the daughter of the German manor lord. In order to regain his love, Liina turns to any means necessary, even if that means tapping into the black magic that is circling around the village. Estonian pagan legends and Christian mythologies come to a spell-binding intersection in NOVEMBER.

programme.title: Nightmare Castle
programme.desc: When a scientist studying the regenerative abilities of blood finds his wife sleeping with the gardener, he murders them both in a fit of rage. When the wife's sister arrives, having inherited the estate, she soon finds herself haunted by a spirit bent on revenge. A sordid tale of madness, murder, lust, greed, and occult magic, Nightmare Castle is director Mario Caiano's wonderfully creepy tribute to Gothic horror.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Dr. Sloan discovers a cure to weight loss in a rare fat-sucking leech. Then, Rose believes she found Champ, the legendary monster of Lake Champlain, but the real monster may still be out there.

programme.title: A Creepshow Animated Special
programme.desc: A surgeon crash-lands on a desert island ... how strong is his will to survive? Then, a teenager's family vacation becomes even more torturous when they pull off the road to see a sinister circus.

programme.title: A Creepshow Holiday Special
programme.desc: Robert Weston has reason to believe he might be a werewolf, but when he goes to a Shapeshifters Anonymous support group, he learns there's something far more sinister than his curse to worry about.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Cassie is being chased by a monster, and endangers a kind stranger when she asks for help- but what can they do? Then, a celebrated photographer is tormented after an award-winning photograph has more consequences than just fame.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A horror novelist's agent offers him help overcoming his writer's block, and the struggling author can't resist. Then, Richard's wife is attacked and becomes a shell of the woman he loved. He'll do anything to help her feel better.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: Lyle is a disappointment, and when his parents meddle in his life and their plan backfires, he must deal with the aftermath. Then, Marcia takes her stepdaughter to her grandmother's house to claim their inheritance, and nothing can stand in her way.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: The Belaskos get more than they bargained for when they move in next door to a vampire-hating suburbanite. Then, a father and son bond by playing an old video game, but soon find out there's a reason it was abandoned.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A young couple are gifted everything they could ever wish for... but there's a catch. Then, an aspiring comic artist keeps hitting roadblocks. As she doodles away her frustrations, she realizes she might have more power than she thinks.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Garden" - Grannie has everyone up at the crack of down to plow the front yard for her garden, while Mr. Drysdale, Miss Hathaway, and a stubborn mule try to stop her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Hair-Raising Holiday" - After Granny cuts off the top of Drysdale and Clyburn's (his doctor) hair for some homegrown remedy, Clyburn tries to keep her from ever doctoring again. Meanwhile, the Clampetts are looking forward to the Possum Day parade, which of course isn't celebrated in Beverly Hills, so Drysdale has to figure out how to have one to keep them happy.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Gets the Misery" - Jed cheers up Granny by pretending to be sick so she can doctor him. Soon Drysdale and his doctor, Clyburn, are subject to her methods as well.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Jethro brings home a friend, Armstrong Dueser McHugh III, who is coddled by a chauffeur who treats him as frail. The Clampetts know better, and show him a good time, friendship and family.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: When Miss Hathaway gets sick and Mr. Drysdale has to speak at a conference, Jed helps out by running the bank and having Elly be his secretary, while Jethro finds a speech that Drysdale accidentally uses instead of his own. All the while a new teller sucks-up to the men and flirts with the women.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Psychiatrist Gets Clampetted" - Jethro finally gets his bill of health from Dr. Twombly so he can graduate the fifth grade, but Twombly really wants to talk to Grannie again. Drysdale brings him to the Clampetts' just as Grannie showed Pearl how to use a love potion, so everyone thinks Twombly's interest in Grannie is love.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Psychoanalyzed" - Jethro needs a health certificate to graduate from the fifth grade, so he goes to the only doctor the Clampetts know about - Mrs. Drysdale's psychiatrist. He gets nowhere with Jethro, so Pearl comes and she thinks he is testing her virtue when he tries to lay her on his couch.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts in Court" - When a couple who ran into the Clampett's car discover that they're rich, they fake injuries and sue the Clampetts.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Entertain" - Jed arranges a party to get Grannie out of her doldrums and invites the Drysdale. But when Mr. Drysdale's boss comes and wants to meet them for dinner, he wonders if he's going to be sent to an Alaskan bank when his boss finds out he's been lying about Jed and his family being sophisticated.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Duke Becomes a Father" - Mlle. Denise comes back for the birth of her dog's puppies, and to see Jed. They do some "courtin' and sparkin'" Mrs. Drysdale isn't nearly so happy when she finds the puppies share more in common with Duke the bloodhound than Claude the poodle.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts and the Dodgers" - Jed and Jethro go golfing with Leo Durocher, coach of the Los Angeles Dodgers, who wants to recruit Jethro as a pitcher when he sees how well he can throw.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Pays His Income Tax" - When an IRS agent gets chased away by Granny, Mr. Drysdale tells him the story of how the Clampetts came to be rich and move to Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Granny's Spring Tonic" - Miss Hathaway's brilliant-but-frumpy bank protegee Gloria is really a sultry gold-digger with eyes for Jed's $34 million, and Jed's just had his annual dose of Granny's spring tonic.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jed Cuts the Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale continues to be forced into doing "practices from the past" by Mrs. Smith-Standish, who discovers that Jed's family is possibly the first to come the country. If so they'd be famous worldwide, but does Jed want that?

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Family Tree" - Mrs. Drysdale wants to get rid of the Clampetts before the arrival of Mrs. Smith-Standish, the head of a 'first family' historical society. But Mrs. Smith-Standish goes to the Clampetts' first and becomes enchanted with their antiques and way of life, even to roping the horrified Mrs. Drysdale into helping do the chores the old-fashioned way.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Sharpshooter Marty Blair tries to persuade Lucas to buy Mark a rifle. After Lucas shoots a man during a robbery at the hotel, Mark and Lucas disagree his character.

programme.desc: Nils Swenson takes the marshal's place for a few days while Micah is away. To maintain peace, Nils insists that anyone coming into town must turn in their weapons.

programme.desc: The first railroad train is coming to the North Fork station. On board is a Japanese nobleman visiting the US.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: At Halloween, rustlers take advantage of the seasonal disguises to raid a cattle ranch. Lucas' faith in his son's honesty is shaken when he hears rumors of Mark being mixed up with the thieves.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Mark and Milly are shocked when Lucas begins gambling. They don't know that Lucas is "undercover" trying to catch a man cheating.

programme.title: The Rifleman

programme.desc: No one is more surprised than Micah when newcomer Leota Carraway claims he's her long-lost husband and the father of her child.

programme.desc: Lucas is torn by conflicting emotions when he meets the town's new preacher, a former outlaw whom the Rifleman vowed to kill.

programme.desc: A former spy moves to Arizona to join a gold robbery, but has a change of heart.

programme.title: Gunmen From Laredo
programme.desc: A jailed rancher escapes to track down the outlaws who framed him for a murder he didn't commit.

programme.title: The Tall T
programme.desc: A stagecoach robbery lands an unwitting rancher in the middle of a standoff with a trio of ruthless thieves.

programme.title: Code of the Fearless
programme.desc: Fred Scott plays a lawman who goes undercover to expose a dangerous gang of criminals in this action-packed drama. He also has time to sing three songs: Cactus Pete, Gonna Ride, and Heres Romance. Screenwriter Fred Myton would go on to write such offbeat features as Nabonga, and Border Phantom.

programme.title: Robin Hood of the Pecos
programme.desc: Roy Rogers finds himself elected sheriff in a wild Texas town after the Civil War. But can Roy handle a place under martial law? And can he save Gabby Hayes from being executed? And how many songs will he get to sing? Clear the burrs from your saddle and get ready for a good one, also starring Marjorie Reynolds, plus Sally Payne as Belle Starr, Queen of the Oklahoma Outlaws!

programme.title: Frontier Justice
programme.desc: Hoot Gibson is back! But he never imagined he'd find his father in an insane asylum and his cattle ranch overrun with sheep! Worst of all, Roger Williams stars as the diabolical James WIlton, a rival who isn't above murder. For trivia buffs: Director Robert F. McGowan was the man behind several of the classic Our Gang comedies...

programme.title: Song of Arizona
programme.desc: This Roy Rogers adventure finds Gabby Whitaker (Gabby Hayes) running a ranch for wayward boys. One of the boys uses stolen money to help Gabby pay off his debts, but things unravel when the boys father, a notorious outlaw named King Blaine, is murdered; the rest of the Blaine gang show up at Gabbys looking for the loot! Director Frank McDonald works with a fine cast, including Dale Evans, Lyle Talbot, and of course, Roys beloved horse Trigger.

programme.title: Jesse James Rides Again
programme.desc: The outlaw Jesse James and his friend are on the run from the law when he is wrongly accused of leading the historic 1876 Northfield, Minnesota bank robbery. With nerve-tensing turns and high-impact action, Jesse rides into a town where a gang is trying to drive area ranchers off their land because there's oil underneath it. Jesse James takes particular interest in the situation of a pretty young woman and her elderly father, whose ranch is being regularly attacked by the gang.

programme.title: Way of the West
programme.desc: Government agent Gordon (Hal Taliaferro) goes undercover to investigate an ongoing war between cattlemen and sheepmen. But he soon finds himself framed for murder! This low budget western from the short-lived Empire Pictures is from the golden era of shoot em ups, when audiences knew who the bad guys were, and couldnt wait for the good guys to serve up some just punishment!

programme.title: Pinkerton
programme.desc: Allan Pinkerton, founder of America's first detective agency, embarks on a mission to capture the notorious outlaw, Jesse James.

programme.title: Doc Holliday
programme.desc: Doc Holliday runs a small dental practice in rural Georgia when he is visited by Wyatt Earp and offered a chance to become a United States Deputy. All Doc has to do is capture the Bridle Brothers - two of most notorious outlaws the West has ever seen. The Doc sets out on an adventure like none other to find these hardened criminals and ends up forging a friendship with Wyatt Earp that would carry into the events of Tombstone.

programme.title: The Undefeated
programme.desc: Unaware that the Civil War has ended, Col. John Henry Thomas (John Wayne) successfully attacks a Confederate company led by James Langdon (Rock Hudson). With the remnants of Langdon's squadron fleeing to Mexico, Thomas and his adopted Native American son, Blue Boy (Roman Gabriel), gather a herd of horses and head for the border with their company, to sell them. After Langdon's caravan is captured by a Mexican general (Tony Aguilar), Thomas nobly sacrifices the horses to free them.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: Harold leaves the lodge in search of a job in the city, while the lodge members all try to replace him.

programme.title: Adventures With Bill
programme.desc: Red and Bill engage in a series of disastrous competitive activities. First, they take part in the Highland Games, where Bill’s attempt at "tossing the caber" goes spectacularly wrong. Then, in a "Hockey Night in Canada" segment, Bill puts on a full set of goaltender gear, only to remove it for a "more mobile" approach, leading to Red firing pucks at him without any padding. Finally, Bill challenges Red to a boxing match, but Red gets Harold involved, causing more chaos.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: The Possum Lake area is struck by a big storm, as predicted by Old Man Sedgwick's body.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: Everyone tries to make money by sending out chain letters.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: Women appreciate creative input; Men appreciate silence. All bets are off when a big American network eyes the Lodge and wants to make some minor changes to the show.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: A dispute over public access to the waterfront is settled - with a tank.

programme.title: Handyman Corner
programme.desc: Red transforms ordinary vehicles into one-of-a-kind contraptions, from a jet-powered car to a convertible Possum Van.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: Stinky Peterson's girlfriend gets stuck.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: The Lodge members decide to attract tourists to the area by putting on the Possum Lodge Water Show.

programme.title: The Red Green Show
programme.desc: Rumors abound at the Lodge when Winston is seen wearing an earring.

programme.title: Red Green - Bonus Material
programme.desc: Some of Red Green's hidden gems, duct-taped together into one collection.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: As the Chewed Up crew wraps up their week at the W South Beach, Miami-born chef Mika Leon brings the most authentically local dish of the entire trip: Pan Con Lechon, the slow-roasted Cuban pork sandwich that anchors Noche Buena, Cuban Christmas Eve. Mika woke up at 4 AM to start the pork shoulderómarinated overnight in a mojo sauce built on sour orange juice, lime juice, orange juice, and 50 cloves of garlic from her grandfather's recipe. After six hours at 350 degrees, the pork is shredded and crisped with additional mojo in a hot pan, then piled high on a special Cuban rollósweeter and fattier than regular bread, made with lard throughout. Mika shares her journey from dabbling in TV production (she quit after a week) to opening a food truck selling $5 Pan Con Lechons at 3 AM to Miami club-goers, which grew into a full restaurant and now a cookbook. Carla notes that the best cooks often learn from family before any formal training, and this dish proves it. The group discusses Miami's cultural identityómore its own country than a Florida cityódefined by Spanglish, its own traffic logic, and immigrant food traditions passed down for generations. Clinton finally admits that despite a full week at the W, none of them ever made it into the pool.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: The South Beach week closes with a live audience tapingóthe most unhinged episode of the tripóas Michael Symon, Carla Hall, and Clinton Kelly perform in front of a crowd of fans. Michael's voice is noticeably shot from a long week of celebrating, so Clinton and Carla carry extra energy while he freestyle hosts from notes he promptly loses within 30 seconds. Carla makes her refreshing Pineapple Lime Agua Fresca: blended fresh pineapple with lime water (lime juice diluted to avoid sharpness), ginger, honey, and wateróa batched mocktail or cocktail mixer that's as beautiful as it is easy. She shares her tip for getting full use of citrus by zesting before juicing. The episode features 'Chew It or Screw It,' a voting game where the audience weighs in on Florida's most adventurous foods: iguana pizza, python sausage, alligator ribs, conch fritters, and fried frog legs. Carla and Clinton report on their spontaneous 7 AM sunrise bike rideótheir first ever togetheróand Clinton jokes about his impossibly pale, non-tanning legs. The trio explores Florida's wildlife peculiarities, from iguanas falling from trees in cold snaps to flying cockroaches given affectionate nicknames. A Chewed Up Challenge heads-up guessing game rounds out the audience interaction with giveaways. Season 3's Miami week ends exactly how it began: loud, funny, and hungry.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome Angie Rito and Scott Tacinelli, the chef-owners behind the celebrated New York restaurant Don Angie, for a lively episode dedicated to Italian American comfort food. The conversation begins with admiration for the restaurant’s iconic lasagna before shifting to the main event: a deeply flavorful, “life-changing” meatball recipe inspired by a blend of both chefs’ grandmothers’ traditions. As Angie and Scott walk through their process, they share chef-driven techniques like using a milk-soaked panade for moisture, puréed onions for even flavor distribution, and roasted garlic to avoid harsh raw bites. The hosts jump in with questions and personal anecdotes, sparking a broader discussion about Italian versus Italian American cooking, rule-breaking in the kitchen, and regional food identity—from Cleveland to New York. The group also plays a spirited round of “Discuss,” debating topics like garlic usage, cheese on seafood, and proper spaghetti etiquette, leading to humorous and opinionated exchanges. Along the way, they highlight the philosophy behind Don Angie’s food: bold, unapologetic flavors built on cheese, garlic, and tradition—without strict adherence to old-world rules. As the meatballs finish baking, they’re tossed in a simple, bright tomato sauce and served family-style, earning rave reviews from the hosts for their rich texture and layered flavor. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway of the chefs’ cookbook and a final celebration of Italian American cuisine, emphasizing creativity, collaboration, and the joy of cooking without limits.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon take on the ultimate comfort food by each creating their own version of a grilled cheese sandwich, showcasing three distinct styles that range from classic to elevated. Michael leads with a rich and decadent Croque Madame, complete with a homemade mornay sauce, gruyère, ham, and a perfectly cooked egg on top, sharing tips on building flavor and texture through technique. Carla follows with a Southern-inspired twist, hollowing out a brioche bun to stuff it with pimento cheese and tangy chow chow pickles, emphasizing balance and texture in every bite. Clinton rounds things out with a fan-inspired take on a classic grilled cheese, combining American, cheddar, and gruyère cheeses on rye bread, using mayonnaise for an evenly crisp, golden exterior. Throughout the episode, the trio trades stories, jokes, and cooking insights, debating everything from sandwich ratios to restaurant culture. They later shift into a lively “overrated vs. underrated” game, diving into food trends, brunch culture, and even aging, blending humor with candid reflections. The episode wraps with a fan giveaway and appreciation for sponsors, leaving viewers inspired to experiment with their own grilled cheese creations at home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon welcome legendary pitmaster Melissa Cookston for a lively, flavor-packed episode centered around Mexican-inspired smoked chicken wings. The conversation kicks off with playful banter about grilling, live-fire cooking, and Melissa’s impressive barbecue credentials, setting the stage for a masterclass in flavor. Melissa walks the hosts through her approach to wings, emphasizing dry rubs over heavy sauces and showcasing a spice blend inspired by her travels to Mexico, featuring garlic, onion, smoked Hungarian paprika, ancho chili powder, cumin, and oregano. Along the way, the group dives into deeper cooking philosophy—trusting your instincts, cooking with your senses, and understanding that great barbecue is about feel as much as technique. Melissa shares insights on smoking temperatures, wood choices, and why over-smoking can ruin a dish, comparing it to over-salting. The energy stays high as Clinton hilariously spirals into wing-induced chaos, while Carla and Michael debate everything from condiments to air fryers. The episode also highlights Melissa’s new cookbook, Fanning the Flames, described as a love letter to her global culinary travels and the people who inspired her. After cooking, the group digs into the wings, followed by a round of “Chew It or Screw It” and fan mail, blending humor with heartfelt moments. The episode wraps with giveaways and a celebration of community, creativity, and the joy of cooking with fire.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: In this “Munch Madness” episode of Chewed Up, Carla Hall, Clinton Kelly, and Michael Symon put eight frozen pizzas through a bracket-style taste test inspired by March Madness. The hosts compare each pairing, breaking down crust, sauce, cheese, and overall flavor while mixing in humor, nostalgia, and brutally honest reactions. As they move through the rounds, the conversation is fueled by strong opinions, unexpected preferences, and plenty of debate about what actually makes a good pizza. Michael’s love of authentic pizza drives passionate critiques, Clinton reflects on his long hiatus from frozen options, and Carla keeps things grounded in everyday practicality for busy households. The competition builds toward a final showdown that sparks lively discussion and a few surprising takes. The episode then shifts into a conversation about potlucks, outdoor dining, and picnics, blending personal preferences with playful arguments. It wraps with a fan giveaway and the hosts’ signature banter.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly welcomes Stacy London for a fun, nostalgic episode focused on creative ways to use leftover hard-boiled eggs. Stacy makes her signature deviled eggs with a creamy filling and bold toppings like chorizo and jalapeño, while Clinton shares a simple asparagus mimosa finished with grated eggs and vinaigrette. Along the way, the hosts swap stories, talk fashion, and play games, bringing humor and energy to an episode that blends easy recipes with playful conversation.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly elevates lunchtime with savory French Onion Soup Tartines, topped with melted Gruyere. While the onions caramelize, the trio discusses their love for French food, debates the perfect early dinner time for getting older, and shares hilarious stories about awkward fan encounters including the time a woman on a crowded train told Clinton he looked "so much fatter in person".

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: It's cozy comfort food season as Michael Symon cooks a hearty Beef and Butternut Squash Stew. While the meat braises, the hosts share their favorite podcasts to listen to while doing chores, universally bash the idea of the "Turducken", and discuss the possibility of a future Chewed Up cookbook.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon celebrates New Year's Eve by sharing his Eastern European family tradition of eating pork and sauerkraut for good luck, making a simple and deeply satisfying Pork and Sauerkraut Stew with pork shoulder, smoked paprika, and beer. The hosts stay in low-key holiday mode, swapping stories about card games, New Year's traditions, and the comfort of staying home.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall rings in the New Year by cooking Black-Eyed Pea Stew with collard greens and rice, a Southern tradition she considers essential for bringing luck, health, and prosperity into the new year. Special guest Deb Riegel, an executive coach and fifth-generation New Yorker, joins to discuss the difference between New Year's resolutions and intentions.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly earns his Gay Soup Diet credentials by making a hearty Chickpea and Ditalini Pasta Soup that he insists is both deeply comforting and genuinely slimming for January. Michael Symon and Carla Hall join in for a lively episode about winter blues, childhood thermostat disputes, and the surprisingly emotional power of a good bowl of soup.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Broccoli Cheddar Twists" - Another day, another attempt by Chris to sneak vegetables into his kids diet. This time, he tries a TikTok recipe for broccoli cheddar twists using frozen puff pastry.

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Crispy Pork Belly Revisited" - The journey to Cantonese-style roasted pork belly continues. This time Chris is trying a longer drying time in the fridge to see if that gets him closer to crisp skin. We’ll see…

programme.title: Recipe Lab with Chris Ying
programme.desc: "Sweet and Sour Pork Ribs" - Chris works his way through "Wayne" aka @FoodieChina888's sweet and sour pork ribs, reminiscent of what he frequently cooks for his kids.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Sogan Dolma (Bosnian Stuffed Onions)" - Chris, Dave, and Rachel take a beautiful, traditional dish of beef-and-rice-stuffed onions braised in tomato sauce, and see how far they can push it.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Spaghetti with Sardines and Cherry Tomatoes" - Rachel Khong joins David Chang and Chris Ying for this episode focused on using tinned fish as the key ingredient. The recipe: Bill Pearis’s Spaghetti with Sardines and Cherry Tomatoes. Dave makes a keto-friendly version. Rachel tries to make it using ingredients from the pantry. Chris makes his vegan.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Pork Belly Jae-Yuk Orzo" - Priya Krishna joins David Chang and Chris Ying to stir up some Korean-Italian magic with Ruth Lee’s Jaeyuk Pork Belly Orzo. Using the key ingredient of canned tomatoes, this is a suprisingly simple, hacky recipe that is delicious and comforting. Priya makes a keto-friendly version. Dave makes his using only shelf-stable ingredients. Chris makes it in under 20 minutes.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Mom's Crab Cakes" - Dave and Chris are joined by Rachel Khong to riff on the Dunn family’s tattoo-worthy crab cake recipe.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Authentic Carne Asada Clásica by Bricia Lopez" - Join renowned chef Bricia Lopez in the kitchen as she shows you how to make the ultimate Carne Asada Clásica! In this step-by-step cooking tutorial, Bricia, known for her expertise in traditional Mexican cuisine, guides you through the process of creating the perfect, mouth-watering carne asada.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: Recipe Club: Gobhi Keema Matar - David Chang and Chris Ying are joined by Priya Krishna in the studio for this special episode of Recipe Club, with cauliflower as the theme ingredient. They try out a recipe for gobhi keema matar, a flavorful curry made with minced cauliflower and peas in a cilantro and onion gravy. Dave attempts to make it in a slow cooker (his method is strongly not recommended to try at home!); Priya makes a “doomsday” version using only shelf-stable ingredients; Chris makes a Tex-Mex version (ever heard of “pea-camole”?).

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Chris Ying Makes Slow Cooker Carne Asada" - Taking inspiration from Bricia Lopez's method for making her LA-style carne asada, Chris tries to make it in a slow cooker instead of on a grill. He uses a chuck roast and lets it cook low and slow in the marinade, coming away with something similar to Carne en su Jugo ("meat in its own juices"), but nonetheless delicious and very easy!

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Parisian Gnocchi" - Dave and Chris are joined by special guest Wylie Dufresne to cook and discuss one of his favorite holiday recipes: Parisian gnocchi! Wylie explains the original recipe, then Dave and Chris describe their self-prescribed fusions for Japanese and Beijing-style fusions, respectively.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Sweet Potato Pie" - Dave and Chris are joined by special guest Malcolm Livingston II to take a deep holiday dive into sweet potato pie. Malcolm shares a remarkable recipe he developed (you will be a holiday hero if you make this!), which Dave tries to make in the microwave in less than 15 minutes, and Chris challenges himself to make handmade agar-agar for his version.

programme.title: Recipe Club
programme.desc: "Cream Cheese - Halifax Donair Dip" - Cream cheese goes beyond the bagel in this episode featuring Mahalia Tahririha’s deconstructed take on a classic Halifax sandwich by way of Greece. David Chang makes a version using only shelf-stable ingredients; Priya Krishna makes hers Keto friendly; and Chris Ying luxe-ifies his, taking a relatively inexpensive recipe and spending more than $150.

programme.title: Recipe Club Recipes
programme.desc: David Chang makes his version of chocolate soufflé for the Valentine's Day episode of Recipe Club. Based on the true story (well, most of the ingredients, but that's about all) of a recipe from sallysbakingaddiction.com, this one turned out to be quite the crowd-pleaser! Dave shook things up by making a matcha crème anglaise, which was a particularly nice touch.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: The pork shoulder, aka Boston butt, gives us Carolina pulled pork. The majestic beef shoulder (yes, there is such a cut) becomes a Texas barbecued beef clod. As for lamb shoulder, Moroccans cook it in a fire-heated underground clay oven to make their legendary mechoui. This show explores the richest, meatiest, most flavorful cut you find in the meat department: the shoulder.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: How about upping your grill game with steaks you may not be familiar with, such as secreto or spinalis dorsi? Secreto is a hyper-flavorful steak cut from a hog’s belly with a grilled vegetable sauce, romesco. Spinalis dorsi the most delectable part of a rib roast reborn as a steak, and grilled with bourbon. And grill a brisket steak fragrant with sizzling shallot sage butter.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: In today’s show, Texas barbecue meets Mexican spice as we explore how American barbecue techniques can enhance three classic Mexican dishes: snapper in pipian in a grilled vegetable and pumpkin seed sauce, pork shoulder pibil smoke-roasted in banana leaves, and a Project Fire first, a dessert quesadilla lavished with bananas and dulce de leche.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Looking back to when our ancestors grilled in the fireplace, on stones around the campfire and directly on the embers, Primal Grilling will make you a barbecue rock star. Try ember-grilled bread roasted vegetable salad, chicken hanging in midair over a smoky fire, and amaretti-stuffed pears grilled on primordial slabs of salt, grilling techniques as old as humankind itself.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Most barbecue enthusiasts can name the big three of barbecue: Kansas City ribs, Carolina pulled pork, and Texas smoked brisket. But what about some of the lesser-known styles? Cornell chicken was created by a Cornell University poultry scientist and today served in upstate New York. Up in Anchorage, Alaska, we have sweet yet smokey barbecued salmon.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Everyone knows that a good barbecue makes the perfect prelude to the game. In this show, we explore how my home state of Florida reimagines three tailgate classics. Get ready for pork shooters stuffed with shrimp, cheese and sausage, wings blasted with fire water, and smoky hamburgers like you’ve never experienced. The secret? Lace them with chopped barbecued brisket.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom learn the secrets behind the perfect traditional English roast dinner in London’s historic Borough Market, explore the best of the diverse food offerings in Brick Lane and pop in to see their friend, Sir Ian McKellen, in his pub in East London.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom share their favorite haunts in Los Angeles, including the famous Mel’s Diner in Santa Monica. They take a trip to Sonoma County for a farm-to-table adventure and get a rare opportunity to take a fascinating glimpse at the world’s most advanced lab-grown meat facility in Oakland.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom journey across Costa Rica from the northern Pacific coast to San José. In Nicoya, a renowned Blue Zone, they uncover dietary secrets linked to long, healthy lives. Venturing into the jungle, they work on a coffee plantation and discover why Costa Rica is the world’s leading exporter of pineapples.

programme.title: Billy & Dom Eat The World
programme.desc: Billy and Dom head to Verbier, one of the world’s most exclusive ski resorts, for a sprinkling of wintery magic and cheese. A lot of cheese. They try Europe’s highest fondue in an igloo on the summit of the Mont-Fort, eat raclette prepared by a robot and learn to bake bread the traditional way in one of the few remaining communal ovens in Switzerland.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: On Mykonos, Diane discovers the quiet life and food of its local farmers, in contrast to the reputation as a dazzling party island. She tastes onion pie, delicious cured pork, and one of the oldest cheeses in the world, kopanisti, still made on the island. In the kitchen, she makes a garlicky local pasta recipe, and a traditional Greek island fish soup.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: All the worlds a stage when you're an amazing home stager. Watch a gorgeous new construction get staged to sell as only Mar can do it. This home references the past, and time travel is definitely a theme.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Our blooper show returns. We work hard to make this show just for you. But it doesn't always go smoothly, and this is your chance to see for yourself. All the crazy antics we didn't show from Season 2.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: You won't believe what I and my team do to this space in the biggest kitchen renovation this season. My neighbor Barbara is in for the kitchen of her dreams. Watch it all unfold.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Our season 2 finale. Mar and his team celebrate their final reveal and all of season 2. The momentum of the entire show season come together for the perfect send-off. Plus we get to meet Daisy, the newest resident of the makeover street.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Paul gets a new client call from people who have just downsized. Their new place is great—except for odd layouts and wasted space. They want Mar's magic touch while they are traveling. Not a small project: a total kitchen and primary bedroom & bath demo and reno in less than a month. Can Mar create the perfect space without the client present? Will they love Mar's Casual Luxury designs?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar continues his rush reno while clients are away. Primary bedroom and bathroom, plus a kitchen—without the client present. A lot is going right—but will it be what they really expect? Despite Mar's award-winning design eye, when the clients return, how highly will they rate these Casual Luxury results?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: A year in the making! Mar goes rogue using short amounts of time between projects this season and decides to do a complete Casual Luxury backyard and Garden Studio redesign and renovation at his own home. With Paul banned from being on the property, can he sneak a peek before the big reveal to Mar's guests? (Stay tuned for a last minute surprise!)

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Sarah and her cat Bodhi have lived in a van for the past two years in western Canada. She gives us a full tour of her classic camper van. Sarah also shares her personal experience about what Vanlife is like for her and why she chose the van that she did.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Electrician builds out a 4x4 sprinter into a modern camper that they are able to both live in and work mobile. This build is done with aluminum and pvc boards, has solar, a composting toilet and everything they need to live comfortably. They also share insights from life on the road.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Virginia lives in her van full-time. She has been traveling North America in her Ford Transit that she designed herself and helped mostly DIY'ers build. Virginia wasn't sure what she was going to do with her retirement until she learned about van life and decided that designing and building out a van to travel and live in was her next creative project.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: John worked with professional van builders to create his high tech sprinter van conversion, equipped with a tiltable solar array, lithium batteries and everything he needs to be a digital nomad and travel in northern Canada. He gives us a tour through his tiny home on wheels and shares what Vanlife means to him.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Amazing DIY camper build with beautiful interior woodwork. This 1988 GMC Vandura went from being an old camper to a tiny house that feels nice and cozy inside like a cabin on wheels. This couple has made this small space their home for over two years.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Storm lives full-time in his DIY Astro Minivan while attending university full-time in one of Canada's most expensive cities. He believes this is a good option for students to save money by not having to pay for rent and using the facilities at school to make van living easier.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Meg and Molly, along with their two cats and one dog, live full-time in a van tiny house that they built from a Montreal fire department utility truck. They have been traveling North America for the past two years. Meg built the van out herself with some help for the solar and plumbing.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Dani tours us around and through his amazing DIY Minivan build that he lives in full time. He spent many hours on this passion project with the idea that the build process should be fun. This van has a full electrical system, a bed and unique storage systems.

programme.title: Alternative Dwellings
programme.desc: Frank and his cat Sunny live in this lifted converted 1998 Chevy Express van. Frank has renovated the build multiple times to fit different needs, and now it is built out to fit just him and his cat. He built it as a 4 seasons camper capable of going off road to explore the less touristy places, with R-14 rockwool insulation, a diesel heater, listed suspension, and upgraded tires.

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes

programme.title: World's Most Secret Homes

programme.title: Under the Pole
programme.desc: Firmly beset in the grip of polar winter, an opportunity arises to study climate change above and beneath the ice sheet. Part scientific exploration, part shared existence with the Inuit, this human adventure reveals the never-before-seen depths of this fascinating world in the throes of upheaval.

programme.title: Secrets of the National Parks
programme.desc: Experience high adventure in "the land of shining mountains." Take an unforgettable hike along the Highline Trail, raft a wild river, and travel by train aboard the renowned Empire Builder. Behold massive glaciers and get close (but not too close!) to bears. Then, relax at a high-altitude chalet.

programme.title: Secrets of the National Parks
programme.desc: By foot, shuttle, and horseback, gain access to the scenic and geological wonders of Zion and Bryce Canyon National Parks. In Zion, embark on an ascent of Angel's Landing. In Bryce, climb sheer cliff walls, tube down the Virgin River, and gaze upon thousands of bizarre rock spires known as hoodoos.

programme.title: Wild Life
programme.desc: Central America is home to some of the most breathtaking animals in the world. In vast and dramatic forests, several dynamic species of animal cohabitate, from the fierce jaguar to the clever monkey.

programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: The east side of California's Sierra Nevada Mountains is home to high deserts and towering peaks. Each fall, this region's aspen groves greet visitors with infinite color enticing landscape photographers from all around the globe.

programme.title: Outside: Beyond the Lens
programme.desc: New Zealand is a landscape cinematographer's dream. In search of the perfect frame, the Beyond the Lens crew travels to South Island where they venture off-the-beaten path and discover a range of exceptional photographic opportunities.

programme.title: Under the Pole
programme.desc: Ghislain and Emmanuelle Bardout and the U.T.P. team take you on a fresh expedition through the Northwest Passage, revealing a mythical seaway that symbolizes the very evolution of the polar world. The team will dive into the "twilight zone" to find the first biofluorescent Arctic species.

programme.title: Lonely Planet: Best Parks Ever - America's National Parks
programme.desc: With acres of sea lions and thousands of buffalo, national parks host the largest homes to all kinds of critters. Get close to your favorite animal in this episode of Best Parks Ever.

programme.title: Taste Hunters
programme.desc: Discover the Piedmont region of Northern Italy, set upon the southern slopes of The Alps. Surrounded by the highest mountain range in Europe, and home to the lush Po Valley, this land contains some of the most fertile earth in Italy.

programme.title: Taste Hunters
programme.desc: Canada is home to crystal lakes, healthy forests, and wide-open spaces. Originally famous for its single-crop farming and the industrialization of certain crops, the concept of local agriculture has taken on new meaning the last few years.

programme.title: Taste Hunters
programme.desc: Located in the southwestern part of the country, the Extremadura region of Spain shares its borders with Portugal. Far from the main tourist trail, Extremadura is a true hidden gem of Spanish culture and is home to luxurious vegetation.

programme.title: Wild Life
programme.desc: A long, mountainous, fascinating passage connects North and South America. Here, hundreds of thousands of species live in one of the richest and most diverse places on earth the Caribbean rainforests.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Animals

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more

programme.title: The Super Simple Show - Space & Planets
programme.desc: This compilation is about Space & Planets! Includes children's songs "Mr. Golden Sun", “Skidamarink” & "More!

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: It’s Finny’s first sleepover and Finny is nervous to sleep at Myrtle the Turtle’s house. Throughout the night, Finny becomes more and more comfortable and after a spooky story, Finny becomes the bravest of everyone at the sleepover! Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: Hello Hello! - Super Simple Puppets and more
programme.desc: Sing and dance along with the Super Simple Puppets to “Hello Hello!”! It’s a classic Super Simple action song for kids.

programme.title: Little Billy's Skateboard - Carl's Car Wash and more
programme.desc: The Bubblegum Bandit pulls another bubblegum burglary and is about to escape when Officer Oona bursts his bubble! Moments later, little Billy skates right into the action and his skateboard becomes a gummy mess! Billy hurries to Carl’s Car Wash, but Carl won't clean his skateboard - it's a CAR wash, not a SKATEBOARD wash! Billy does some quick thinking...and his skateboard might just go through the car wash after all!

programme.title: 10 Little Dinosaurs - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: 10 Stegosauruses! 10 Triceratopses! 10 Pterodactyls! 10 Brontosauruses! And 10 Tyrannosaurus Rex! Wow! This Super Simple counting song introduces the numbers 1-10 and also some very cool dinosaur names! Can you say them all?!

programme.title: Steve's Street Sweeper - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Steve the Street Sweeper is cleaning the street when garbage splatters all over the side of his truck! He doesn't want a dirty truck, so Steve races to Carl’s Car Wash! With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Steve's vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Steve leaves one happy customer, and is ready to get back to sweeping rubbish.

programme.title: Down By The Bay - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see cat wearing a hat? How about a goat rowing a boat? Well you can in this video for the classic children’s song, Down By The Bay!

programme.title: Mr. Pig & Mr. Mouse Have A Mess On Their Hands - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig love making food and having their friends over to eat it. But afterwards, their house is a complete mess! It's so gross, they don't even want to drive their van home to clean up. How can Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig share their love of food with their friends without cluttering their home? Mr. Monkey has a tasty idea! What is it? Watch and find out!

programme.title: Finny The Shark - Down In The Bay and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see a shark glowing in the dark? Did you ever see a stingray dancing at the ballet? Finny and Mama Shark are singing a rhyming song about all of their silly friends down in the bay! Sing along!

programme.title: Driving In My Car - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Drive a car, ride a bike, row a boat, ride on a train, and fly an airplane with this playful and super fun song for kids; “Driving In My Car”! Driving in my car. Vroom vroom vroom. Driving very fast. Vroom vroom vroom. Driving very slow. Vroom vroom vroom. I like driving. Driving in my car.

programme.title: See You Later Alligator - Super Simple Puppets - Kids Songs - Super Simple
programme.desc: It’s time to go! Here’s a video featuring the Super Simple Puppets for our popular goodbye song, See You Later, Alligator. This is a fun song to sing at the end of a class, party, or play-date!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Haruto's robot Metatron breaks down, activating villain Titan. Meanwhile, Jett and Marty build a rollercoaster and use magnets to reroute a stalled mile-long train.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Super Space Scouts and Jett, Matthew, and Dizzy chase a giant ball through a black hole. They save Earth from a moon-sized ball and deliver it to Mars.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Chloe and Dad escape a pterosaur world with a special glider after a time slip. Jett swaps roles, causing chaos with an inflatable duck, and learns teamwork.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Super Wings take Kamal and Nadia to the dinosaur era for a sports showdown, revealing all dinosaurs' strengths. In Scotland, they fix an inflatable playground for exhausted Nessie.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Lucie clean Amara's dad's underwater base and help an injured shark. Julian's safety gear interview reveals a crane's trouble and a forklift's heroics.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy help Louis with a chaotic tennis match and AI machine, leading to his success. Jett and Lucie recover a lost golden record from an alien spacecraft, sparking joy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: At Noah’s park, Camira hunts block dino eggs with Super Wings, facing surprises when Golden Girl snatches the prize and the egg escapes! Powered by new pod gear, Camira triumphs, while Jett braves the Sahara to find Latu and a fennec fox.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy jet to Jeju, helping Taehoon’s robot save Grandma’s shellfish from a giant fish, then team up to free it. On Mars, Jett and Marty chase Roy's lost bags, while Jinga dazzles with an out-of-this-world tour.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Dizzy join Hazel and Runi in Australia to help animals escape wildfires, then fly to Seoul where tiny Antny enters the Strongest Bug Contest, faces rampaging rivals, and, with Super Wings’ upgrades, helps save the city.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty help wash Maxy the giant plane and calm geysers at Oyunuma Pond, then deliver a Wright Flyer to Braden, sparking a time-hop to the Wright brothers, where teamwork lifts spirits and the Flyer takes flight.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett, Lucie, and Runi’s friendly contest becomes teamwork when they rescue toys from a Vietnam factory fire. Soon, Jett races to Dino World, freeing stranded friends and reuniting a T-Rex family as quakes rumble. Super Wings deliver!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Noah and the Dino Wings turn hail into fruit seeds, planting a thriving farm with Jett’s help and saving Dino World from a flood. Later, teamwork and a daring rescue inside Supersaurus bring the friends closer as everyone returns home safe.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Sicily, Jett and Curie foil invisibility pranks and stop a wild volcano eruption with Curie’s snow launcher, teaching Renata as chaos turns to learning. Aboard Ned’s ship, Super Electric Suits help them rescue everyone from a junk robot.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While the World Spaceport celebrates Jimbo’s retirement, Jett faces a UFO and Space Monster, piloting World Robot as chaos erupts. The team unites, time freezes, and Jimbo reveals Megamon is an old friend, choosing to stay with everyone.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and the Super Wings liven up Xiang Xiang’s Tanghulu shop, rescuing everyone from a syrupy mess and drawing big crowds. In Dino World, the Dino Wings halt a Dinobase takeover and reunite a wandering pterosaur hatchling with its mom.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Floods and thunder strike Dino World but Trico overcomes his fear to help Jett and the Dino Wings rescue every dino with paper boats and planes. Later Noah and Mom’s dino costume adventure leads to a mix-up with real maiasauras, and families reunite.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Between 12 and 24 months, little ones become increasingly interested in more complex ideas and begin to explore numbers and counting. Numbers Nursery introduces little ones to numerals 1 through 5 in a captivating, interactive way that invites them to join in, clap their hands and count out loud.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Hop aboard to see vehicles that travel by land, water and sky! See real-life images of cars, boats and planes in this on-the-go adventure that features cameos from some of your favorite Baby Einstein characters, all while listening to music from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become better acquainted with the world, they grow curious about animals of all kinds, beyond just the family pet. And as their communication skills continue to develop, they enjoy imitating animal sounds as well. Baby Noah Animal Expedition takes your little explorer across the globe to meet animals -- two-by-two -- from the savannah, the rainforest and tropics, the Outback, the ocean and the Polar Regions.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Babies love animals, and now you can bring animals from the seven continents home to your baby. Meet dozens of exotic animals including Asian pandas, Australian kangaroos and Arctic snow monkeys...all filmed in their beautiful, natural habitats. Rhythms and songs inspired by faraway lands have been orchestrated especially for little ears and encourage your baby to sing, dance and play to the world beats. You'll laugh together at the funny puppet shows featuring moms and their baby animals.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As babies develop, their understanding of the world, and how its pieces "fit" together, continues to progress. DISCOVERING SHAPES playfully introduces five basic forms circle, oval, triangle, square and rectangle as each appears in the context of toys, nature and everyday objects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Super Supremes the Bath Song & More Kids Songs
programme.desc: Time for baby to take a bath! Bath time has never been so much fun! Enjoy this really fun kids song as baby takes his bath.

programme.title: Baby Bao Panda Non-Stop
programme.desc: Baby Bao Panda non-stop playlist with fun & kids song videos. Children will watch and learn our other popular & kids friendly songs like row row your boat, jack and jill, three little kittens, ten in the bed, incy wincy spider and more cartoon rhymes and songs for babies.

programme.title: Comedy Cartoon Videos - Deviled Eggz
programme.desc: Yolk's on you! In this zany, hilarious world, some very devilish eggz are always pulling pranks on each other. In their kitchen, these deviled eggz like to compete to be the best. They are sure to crack you (and maybe themselves) up! This silent slapstick comedy cartoon series has over the top situations bound to amuse and entertain the kids and adults alike.

programme.title: Kids Transport Song by Super Supremes
programme.desc: Jeff and Jen, the Super Supremes parents teach the kids about transportation through a fun upbeat song that you will definitely enjoy and learn something new too.

programme.title: Baby Shark & More Kids Songs (Little Treehouse)
programme.desc: Let's explore the ocean with Baby Shark, Mommy Shark, Daddy Shark, Grandma Shark and Grandpa Shark.

programme.title: Little Eddie Popular Rhymes
programme.desc: Learning is always fun with Little Eddie popular nursery rhymes. We bring to you some amazing songs for kids to sing along with us and have a good time. Kids will dance, laugh, sing and play along with our videos while they also learn numbers, letters, colors, good habits and more!

programme.desc: Everyone is frightened because some visiting birds keep trying to sit on their heads. According to Chirpee, the birds just enjoy sitting on people's heads. programme.desc: The friends are very tired because Mr. Moon hasn't come to sing them their nightly lullaby. Thunderbell got trapped in a giant bubble in the middle of a bubble bath. In order to find out a way to bring Thunderbell back to earth, Rainbow Ruby turns into a scientist.

programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Gina accidentally broke Felicia's house, leaving Felicia homeless. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby
programme.desc: Thunderbell is sad because he's discovered a new fruit and eaten all of it. Now there's none left and he'll never be able to eat that fruit again. programme.title: Rainbow Ruby

programme.desc: It's winter and the pond's frozen and that means Strawberry and friends can ice skate! They decide to showcase their talents at an Ice Dance Show. But just then, a new kid named Apricot shows up.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Detective BJ is on the case when several sports balls suddenly disappear. Determined to find the mysterious ball thief, BJ falsely accuses Riff of being the thief. BJ learns he cannot jump to conclusions and must find the whole story to see who the thief really is.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Celebrating her birthday in the park with friends, Layla is more interested in hot dogs than her mother's Middle Eastern food. When Barney reads the story of Aladdin and the Magic Lamp, Layla is transported to a Middle Eastern marketplace where she learns different cultures have many exciting foods and customs to offer.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Captain Pickles and Wonder Princess both wanted to be the super-est superheroes. When Captain Pickles said he could jump over the moon, everyone wanted to see. BJ knew he couldn't really jump that high, but he could jump high enough to save Emma's balloon. Everyone was proud of him, even if he couldn't jump over the moon. BJ was a hero.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney comes up with the idea to have a sports day in the park. All day, they play games like soccer and hopscotch among others, all for fun. Meanwhile, Baby Bop meets a girl with special needs named Jill, as well as her mom. As Baby Bop gets to know Jill more, she encourages her to play hopscotch with the others. In the end, Baby Bop and Jill win the trophy for hopscotch and she and her mother leave.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: After the kids judge a spider by its creepy looks, Barney tells them the story of The Reluctant Dragon where a shepherd became friends with a dragon, who was judged by her appearance.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Baby Bop's friend Monty the butterfly passes through the park with a flock of friends on their way to Mexico. When Monty gets left behind, Barney takes everyone on a visit to Mexico to return Monty to his insect friends and family and give Baby Bop the chance to say goodbye to her butterfly friend.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney's excited to find out that Rusty's family is adopting a new baby sister from China; however, Rusty's not sure if he's going to be a good brother, and he doesn't know much about China. Barney shows Rusty and the others some info China on the computer and BJ and Baby Bop talk about siblings. Through Barney's Travel Book, Barney takes Rusty, BJ and Baby Bop on an adventure to China where he can learn more about his new sister's heritage. When they get there, the people are celebrating Chinese New Year. From lion dances and talented performers to tasty treats, making lanterns and puppet shows, the celebration never runs out of fun. By the time he returns to the park, Rusty excitedly meets his new baby sister, named Mei Ling, and realizes he will be the best big brother ever.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Ben is late to the park and is disappointed to realize he missed his chance at riding a real pony. He has waited his whole life for this, and now it looks as if hes missed his opportunity. Luckily, Barney takes Ben to Spain where they meet Regalo, a dancing horse with Riff and Baby Bop. Ben and Barney return to the park just in time for him to ride the pony after all! Ben is thrilled to ride a horse and learned an important lesson about patience and waiting.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Emma is stuck with a school project and can't play soccer with Joshua. Having to interview her parents and get info about an interesting ancestor, she is frustrated with the assignment. Barney helps her discover that her ancestors were from Venice, Italy. Using his Travel Book, Barney takes Joshua, Emma, and Riff to Venice, Italy. He gives his friends a gondola ride as well as telling them about famous explorer Marco Polo. Barney's friend, Anna Maria tells Emma that she knew her great grandfather and takes her to his shop, where they try on masks and make some of their own. Emma learns that if she makes the mask in parts, she'll get it done. Barney reminds her of this lesson, so she will do the same for her school project.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Barney, Baby Bop, and BJ are helping the kids get ready for a school play. Barney tells everyone that putting on shows began in Greece. Of course, the little dinos and kids want to visit the country, so off they go! While there, they meet some friends and decide to put on a play in an amphitheatre. Barney knows a great story that originated in Greece called King Midas. By the end of the day, everyone has learned that wanting too much is not a good thing, especially BJ!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When Ben accidentally includes Kioko's prized music box from Switzerland in a cardboard box of yard sale items-the music box is sold! Kioko is sad and Ben wants to make things right, so he and Barney travel to Switzerland in search of a replacement gift. While there, they sample Swiss food, customs and games-but no music box! Luckily when Ben returns the music box is returned!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: When the kids find a mysterious, colorful egg in the park, Barney takes them on a trip to Kenya to try to find out what kind of animal could have laid the egg. While in Kenya, Barney and the kids meet Ayira who introduces them to many people, cultures, and animals of the country!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Ben and Rusty are looking for items for superhero costumes, when Barney comes to life, wearing a tutu. Tracy comes in and explains that she needs to find a bird of paradise for a school project. Ben, unsure, tells her they're only found in Hawaii and what they look like. Barney then takes the kids to Hawaii, using his Travel Book. In Hawaii, they learn different words, how to make leis and even a hula dance. Meanwhile, Ben and Rusty search for the bird, with a few close calls (a feather and what ended up being a coconut). During the Luau, it's revealed that the Bird of Paradise is actually a flower, not a bird. Ben, believing that it was a bird earlier, is shocked to hear that and apologizes to Rusty for getting him caught up in things. In the end, it all ends up just fine.

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: Emma wants to paint an award-winning painting for the Art In The Park Show, but she isn't satisfied with her artwork. Barney takes her to France to learn how to paint from his friend Pierre. While there, they learn about France. And Emma learns how to paint by following her heart!

programme.title: Barney and Friends
programme.desc: It's Earth Day, a great time for everyone to learn how to help take care of our world, but Ben is wasting paper while BJ isn't too concerned about cutting down a tree to make room for a new playground. After a trip to the rainforest and a meeting with Mother Nature, everyone learns the importance of taking care of our world. As Earth Day comes to an end, Barney reminds us all that taking good care of our Earth is the natural thing to dofor the people we love.
